narrative: The credit-scoring API was launched into the EU market in Q1 with no notified-body involvement and no CE marking, despite clearly falling within Annex III §5(b). The Annex IV technical file consists of a four-page README, and no Art 10 data governance statement was produced — the team could not state whether the training data was representative across nationality or age. Adverse credit decisions are issued automatically with no human review path and no Art 22 GDPR meaningful information surfaced to the data subject. System logs are rotated every 30 days, well below the Art 12 retention floor. The underlying GPAI foundation model is used in production with no Art 53 transparency summary on file, and no model card is published. A reported wave of discriminatory outcomes from a hospital deployer was not escalated to market surveillance, and the ISO 42001 AI policy remains an unsigned draft from the prior year.
